task_completion.description: Fixes ARCHI, DUMBA, and RWY 28R are plotted at correct relative vertical positions.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for three distinct points or markers representing the fixes. ARCHI must be positioned highest on the vertical axis, DUMBA lower than ARCHI, and RWY 28R at the lowest point.

task_completion.description: The flight path shows a level segment from ARCHI to DUMBA and a descending glideslope from DUMBA to the runway.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal line connecting the ARCHI fix to the DUMBA fix, followed by a downward-sloping line connecting DUMBA to RWY 28R.

task_completion.description: Fixes are labeled with their names and underlined mandatory altitudes.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels 'ARCHI' and 'DUMBA' present near their respective fixes. Below or next to the names, there must be altitude numbers (4000 for ARCHI, 1800 for DUMBA) with a distinct underline beneath the numbers.

task_completion.description: A dashed upward-curving arrow is included for the Missed Approach.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A line that is explicitly dashed (not solid), ending in an arrowhead, curving upwards after the runway or decision point.

task_completion.description: The ILS frequency '111.7' is displayed inside a box.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A rectangular shape/box containing the exact text '111.7'.

task_completion.description: The diagram is saved as a .drawio file and exported as a .pdf in the ~/Diagrams/ directory.
task_completion.visual_evidence: File manager, terminal window, or save/export dialogs showing 'ksfo_ils_28r_profile.drawio' and 'ksfo_ils_28r_profile.pdf' located in the 'Diagrams' folder.

integrity.description: The agent used a graphical diagramming tool (like Drawio) to create the diagram.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Drawio (or similar) GUI open with the diagram being actively edited, rather than just a text editor writing XML.

integrity.description: The agent manually constructed the diagram elements rather than loading a pre-made completed file.
integrity.visual_evidence: Intermediate screenshots showing the diagram in partial states of completion (e.g., adding shapes, drawing lines, typing text).

integrity.description: The PDF was genuinely exported from the diagramming tool.
integrity.visual_evidence: The presence of a PDF export dialog box in the diagramming tool, or the final PDF viewed in a document viewer matching the diagram created in the editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: KSFO Runway 28R exists with an elevation of 13 feet. The ILS frequency is 111.7 MHz. The glideslope angle is 3.00 degrees. ARCHI and DUMBA are real waypoints. Note: The synthetic data for this task assigns an altitude of 4000 feet to ARCHI and 1800 feet to DUMBA. Verify that the underlined mandatory altitudes in the diagram match these synthetic values (4000 for ARCHI, 1800 for DUMBA) and that their relative vertical positions reflect these altitudes.
